{{Rewrite|Generally lackluster execution.|date=4/26/26}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 11: Wentworth Gas}}{{Foreground|File=Backrooms_foggy_overlay.png|Opacity=0.7}} {{Glacier Theme}} {{Object_Class|logo=
[[File:monochrome_foggy_br_logo.png|75px|center]]
[[File:foggy_br_logo.png|75px|center]]
|utility=5|risk=3|properties='''Dangerous''' / '''Highly inflammable''' / '''Magnetic''' / '''Odourless'''}} ==Description== {{Simple Image Block|image=backrooms_wentworth_gas.jpg|caption=Wentworth gas found in a room in [[Level 3]]|align=right}} '''Wentworth Gas''' is a mystical and dangerous chemical found in air that is known to impair a wanderer's health. It looks foggier than regular water vapour, but is completely odourless. It is believed to be highly magnetic and usually found in areas with strong electromagnetic fields, such as powered electric engines. Its '''L.D.50''' was found to be 560 mg/kg, slightly more dangerous than methamphetamine from [[the Frontrooms]]. The condensation point for '''Wentworth Gas''' is 253 K (-20 °C). This implies that in cold-temperature levels, '''Wentworth Gas''' could naturally be found as in liquid state. Understandably, none has been found yet, mainly because of the difficulties in studying such cold levels. Its freezing point is 173 K (-100 °C). None has been found, either. ==Symptoms== Symptoms can occur after high exposure to '''Wentworth Gas'''. They may vary depending on the patient, but they do not go beyond listed below: *Urine discolourationThis is due to liver failure. The urine can be very yellow or even a bit brown. *Dizziness *Fatigue *Nausea *Tiredness *Feeling hot *Loss of appetite *Vomiting (though very rarely) ==Etymology== The name is an eponym, named after Rhonda Wentworth. Rhonda Wentworth is not the discoverer; however, she found and reported the first affected wanderers. ==Usage== '''Wentworth Gas''' turns out to be a good source of fuel. It is inflammable. In its compressed liquefied state, it holds 30 MJ/kg. There have though been no known cases of communities creating a factory or any kind of engine to try to use Wentworth Gas. For now, Wentworth Gas is being contained in tight glass jars by local settlements. ==History== On '''30 April 2019''', eight wanderers (seven female and one male) reported urine discolouration, dizziness and nausea to the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Department of Clinical Medicine]]. For six days, the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Backrooms Red Front]] could not find the cause, however their patients' symptoms had got slightly better. The patients were sent over to the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Department of Biomedical Research]] to study their bodies. Maliciously, an unknown medical specialist blamed their symptoms on their age (though the entire group was in their 30s)What the medical specialist did was fraudulent. It is very obvious. The group of wanderers meant to get this appealed, but could not due to personal reasons.. '''17 April 2019''', '''Rhonda Wentworth''' found the same eight wanderers' cadavers in [[Level 154|Station 154]], which she was surprised by, because the level does not have any dangers. '''5 December 2020,''' Rhonda Wentworth began another investigation at [[Backrooms Red Cross|Department of Biomedical Research]]. It was not actually begun until 2021, so their bodies had to be preserved. '''11 April 2021''', the medical specialists discovered some amounts of an unidentified chemical in the wanderers' lungs. After some tests, its '''L.D.50''' was discovered to be very low. They spread the word. The next day, [[Backrooms Today]] wrote an in-depth article about the story, and B.T. also included safety precautions around Wentworth gas to its viewers. {{Quote|..If one ever ventures into Dub-GasAn abbreviated portmanteau form of Wentworth Gas pervasive territory, it is strictly advised to don respiratory protection to safeguard against the inhalant, which is most prevalent lurking near electrical equipment as a thick white fog.
''An excerpt from a B.T. article posted on its international branch, dubbed "W-Gas and why you should be concerned''
}} '''10 June 2021''', Scientist '''Brice Anthonyson''' discovered the chemical's high flammability and reports it as a good source of fuel. [[Backrooms Today]] wrote an article about Anthonyson's discovery the day after. By '''30 July 2021''', The [[Backrooms Red Cross|Red Front]] reported having had 100 patients affected with Wentworth Gas so far. According to the [[Backrooms Red Cross|Department of Medicine]], staying hydrated, lying in bed and taking care of oneself is quite a reliable way to get better. Fortunately, apart from the first eight wanderers discovered by '''Rhonda Wentworth''', there have not been any more deaths. One week later, the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organisation]] started at investigation investigating where the gas comes from, why it appeared so suddenly and if there is any machine generating the Gas or if there is a limited supply. '''14 April 2022''', an anonymous member of the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organisation]] invented the [[Detector of Wentworth gas|Wentworth Gas Detector]]. He/she chooses not to say where he hid it. His/her motives to be secretive are unclear, and some are speculating whether it is true or not. ==Dos and Don'ts== ===Dos when you find yourself in a space containing high amounts of Wentworth Gas === *Hold your breath and flee *Wear a mask *Get medical attention *Report the space to an expert to clear the area and secure it for later use (or simply dispose of it) *Inform the [[The Kauer Research Organization|Kauer Research Organisation]] about where you found Wentworth Gas ===Don'ts when you find yourself in a space containing high amounts of Wentworth Gas=== *Stay in the area * Try to capture it yourself unless you are an expert * Light any type of fireThe gas is highly inflammable. It might start a critical fire. *Dismiss any symptoms {{Footnotes}}
